verdict: All three posts carry genuine operational depth and translate Clear's framework into VRM-specific behavior accurately. The posts avoid the book-report structure, dismantle real operator misconceptions, and include concrete examples. The newsletter extends meaningfully into the two uncovered laws and builds a deployable implementation.
strengths: Post 1's identity contrast (identity violation vs. acceptable delay) is sharp and immediately recognizable to the target audience
Post 2's habit stack formula is presented concretely with VRM-specific examples, not just explained in theory
Post 3's two-concept structure (two-minute rule and never miss twice) works well because they address sequential failure modes
The newsletter's four-law audit is a genuine tool, not abstract summary, and the marginal gains application to appointment quality is original and useful
No em dashes present across all files
CTA footer intact on all posts
Field notes are type-specific and add information rather than restating the body
problems: Post 2's header ('WANTING TO PROSPECT MORE CONSISTENTLY IS NOT THE SAME AS BUILDING A PROSPECTING HABIT') is accurate but slightly long for a LinkedIn title line — it reads more as a thesis than a hook
